requiredExam.label: Assess lower leg edema

source.source_section: Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication. Edema changes medication interpretation and volume-sensitive blood pressure management.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect and press over both lower legs or dependent areas for pitting, symmetry, tenderness, and proximal extent.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When hypertension, renal disease, or medication effects could affect volume status
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Edema changes medication interpretation and volume-sensitive blood pressure management.
requiredExam.limitations: Assess lower leg edema is supportive for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Test chair-rise strength

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate hypokalemic weakness or functional impairment from mineralocorticoid excess. Weakness changes hypokalemia severity assessment, ECG/lab urgency, and treatment safety.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets hypokalemic weakness or functional impairment from mineralocorticoid excess; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).
requiredExam.technique: Ask the patient to rise from a chair without using arms if safe, observing proximal strength and need for assistance.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When hypokalemia, cramps, or weakness is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hypokalemic weakness or functional impairment from mineralocorticoid excess
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Weakness changes hypokalemia severity assessment, ECG/lab urgency, and treatment safety.
requiredExam.limitations: Test chair-rise strength is supportive for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Auscultate heart rhythm

source.source_section: Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate hypokalemia-related arrhythmia, severe hypertension complication, or ECG-relevant cardiac rhythm abnormality. Irregular rhythm changes ECG need, potassium urgency, medication safety, and monitored-care threshold.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets hypokalemia-related arrhythmia, severe hypertension complication, or ECG-relevant cardiac rhythm abnormality; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).
requiredExam.technique: Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When primary aldosteronism, hypokalemia, palpitations, or severe hypertension is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hypokalemia-related arrhythmia, severe hypertension complication, or ECG-relevant cardiac rhythm abnormality
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Irregular rhythm changes ECG need, potassium urgency, medication safety, and monitored-care threshold.
requiredExam.limitations: Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Palpate abdomen for tenderness

source.source_section: Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) curated endocrine workup: conditional bedside maneuver add-ons

conditionalExam.action: Add when adrenal crisis, vomiting, abdominal pain, shock is present; this evaluates abdominal complication, endocrine crisis mimic, or precipitating illness. Tenderness changes adrenal crisis severity and mimic evaluation.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets abdominal complication, endocrine crisis mimic, or precipitating illness; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect and palpate the abdomen gently, localizing tenderness and stopping if guarding, rebound, or severe pain occurs.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When adrenal crisis, vomiting, or abdominal pain is possible
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: abdominal complication, endocrine crisis mimic, or precipitating illness
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Tenderness changes adrenal crisis severity and mimic evaluation.
conditionalExam.limitations: Palpate abdomen for tenderness is supportive for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration

source.source_section: Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) curated endocrine workup: conditional bedside maneuver add-ons
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.action: Add when vomiting, dehydration, poor intake, polyuria, hyperglycemia is present; this evaluates volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion. Dehydration changes fluid strategy, lab urgency, and monitoring needs.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When vomiting, poor intake, polyuria, hyperglycemia, hypercalcemia, or hypernatremia is present
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Dehydration changes fluid strategy, lab urgency, and monitoring needs.
conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.
